Figures included in visual abstract

Lucien Bickerstaff

2026-02-03

# Seed for reproducibility
set.seed(2025)

# Libraries
library(here)

Panel E

Normalised SPDs of primaries used

# Import spectral data
spec_data <- read.xlsx(here("data", "20251105_corrected_settings.xlsx"))
# Transpose the data
spec_data <- as.data.frame(t(spec_data), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Assign the first row as header
colnames(spec_data) <- spec_data[1, ]
spec_data <- spec_data[-1, , drop = FALSE]

Extract spectral data

spec_data <- spec_data %>%
  select(c(Name, "380":"780"))

spec_data <- spec_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -1,                        # all columns except the first
    names_to = "wavelength",          # column for wavelength names
    values_to = "Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]"           # column for measured values
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(  # ensure numeric x-axis
    -1,  # skip the first column (the names)
    ~ as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", .))
  )) %>% 
  mutate(
    measurement = str_extract(Name, "(?<=#)\\d+"),
    measurement = factor(measurement),
    measurement = fct_reorder(measurement, as.numeric(as.character(measurement))),
    measurement_num = as.numeric(measurement)
  ) %>%
  select(-Name)
gradient <- t(w_length2rgb(400:700))
g <- rasterGrob(gradient, width = unit(1, "npc"), height = unit(1, "npc"), interpolate = TRUE)

# Manual peak wavelengths (from the product manual)
primary_wavelengths <- c(
  "2" = 445,
  "3" = 465,
  "4" = 475,
  "5" = 505,
  "6" = 525,
  "7" = 545
)

# Label positions using manual peak wavelengths
label_data <- data.frame(
  primary    = names(primary_wavelengths),
  wavelength = as.numeric(primary_wavelengths),
  label_y    = 1.08
)

# Gradient for line colours
wavelength_seq <- 380:780
spectrum_cols <- sapply(wavelength_seq, w_length2rgb)

gradient_scale <- scale_colour_gradientn(
  colours = spectrum_cols,
  values  = scales::rescale(wavelength_seq),
  name    = "Wavelength (nm)"
)

plot_data <- spec_data %>% 
  mutate(
    primary = case_when(
      measurement_num == 28 ~ "2",
      measurement_num == 32 ~ "3",
      measurement_num == 36 ~ "4",
      measurement_num == 40 ~ "5",
      measurement_num == 44 ~ "6",
      measurement_num == 48 ~ "7"
    )
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(measurement_num %in% c(28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48)) %>% 
  group_by(primary) %>% 
  mutate(Ee_norm = `Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]` / max(`Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]`))

plot_spectra <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = wavelength, y = Ee_norm, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "darkred") +
  
  # Vertical dashed lines at manual peak wavelengths
  geom_segment(
  data = data.frame(wavelength = primary_wavelengths),
  aes(x = wavelength, xend = wavelength, y = 0, yend = 1),
  linetype = "dashed",
  colour = "black",
  alpha = 0.6,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # Manual labels at manual peak wavelengths
  geom_text(
    data = label_data,
    aes(x = wavelength, y = label_y, label = primary),
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  gradient_scale +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    limits = c(0, 1.08),
    labels = function(x) ifelse(x %% 1 == 0, formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 0), x)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Wavelength (nm)",
    y = "Relative irradiance",
    title = "Spectral distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 25, l = 10), # need to avoid clipping on right-hand side
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 5))
    ) +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  plot_formatting
plot_spectra

Hypothesis plots

Need to simulate data. We run the simulation once again (same as for BFDA)

m <- readRDS(here("outputs", "emg_lmm.rds"))

design_matrix <- read_csv2(here("outputs", "design_matrix.csv"))
## ℹ Using "','" as decimal and "'.'" as grouping mark. Use `read_delim()` for more control.
## Rows: 24 Columns: 13
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ";"
## chr  (1): color
## dbl (12): primary, lab_visit, Irradiance [W/sqm] (380-780nm), Illuminance [l...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# need to compute log10 mel EDI to generate the data using the model,
# which itself has log10 mel EDI as predictor
design_matrix <- design_matrix %>% 
  mutate(melanopic_edi_log10 = log10(calculated_MEDI)) %>% # change to true measured mel EDI!
  mutate(photopic_lux_log10 = log10(calculated_Ev)) # change to true measured illuminance!

# Extend it to have all participants
design_matrix <- design_matrix %>%
  crossing(subject = factor(1:20)) %>% 
  rename(participant_id = subject)

design_matrix <- design_matrix %>% 
  mutate(is.zero = calculated_MEDI == 0,
         log_mel_edi = pmax(calculated_MEDI, 1) %>% log10())

simulate_from_design <- function(design_df, model, n_subjects = 20, sd_scaling = 1) {
  # Load necessary package on worker nodes if using parallel
  if (!"MASS" %in% loadedNamespaces()) library(MASS)
  if (!"lme4" %in% loadedNamespaces()) library(lme4)
  
  design_df$is.zero <- as.numeric(design_df$is.zero)
  
  # Extract LMM parameters
  beta        <- fixef(model)                        # fixed effects
  vc          <- VarCorr(model)$participant_id       # random intercept variance
  sd_intercept <- sqrt(vc[1, 1])                     # scalar
  sd_resid     <- sigma(model) * sd_scaling          # residual SD on log10 scale
  
  # Generate random intercepts for each subject
  # subjects <- unique(design_df$participant_id)
  all_subjects <- unique(design_df$participant_id)
  subjects <- all_subjects[1:n_subjects] # restrict to a number of participants
  design_df <- design_df %>% 
    dplyr::filter(participant_id %in% subjects)
  
  re <- rnorm(
    n = length(subjects),
    mean = 0,
    sd = sd_intercept
  )
  
  re_df <- data.frame(
    participant_id = subjects,
    re_intercept   = re
  )
  
  # Merge design matrix with random intercepts
  dat <- design_df %>%
    dplyr::left_join(re_df, by = "participant_id") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # linear predictor on log scale
      eta_log = beta[1] + beta["is.zeroTRUE"] * is.zero + beta["log_mel_edi"] * log_mel_edi + re_intercept,
      
      # add noise on log10 scale
      y_log10 = rnorm(n(), mean = eta_log, sd = sd_resid),
      
      # bring back to original response scale
      response_sim = 10^y_log10
    )
  
  return(dat)
}

sim_dat <- simulate_from_design(design_df = design_matrix, model = m, sd_scaling = 1)
sim_dat_plot <- sim_dat %>% 
  rename(illuminance = `Illuminance [lx] (CIE1931 2°)`,
         medi = `X v,mel,D65 (MEDI) [lx]`) %>% 
  mutate(primary = as.factor(as.numeric(primary)), # Reset factor labels to 1-6 instead of 2-7
         wavelength = primary_wavelengths[primary]) %>% # add wavelength per primary 
  group_by(primary, lab_visit) %>% 
  summarise(mean_response = mean(response_sim, na.rm = TRUE),
            # mean_response_norm  = mean_response / max(response_sim, na.rm = TRUE),
            illuminance = first(illuminance), 
            medi = first(medi),
            wavelength = first(wavelength),
            .groups = "drop")

label_dat <- sim_dat_plot %>%
  group_by(primary, wavelength) %>%
  do({
    fit <- lm(mean_response ~ illuminance, data = .)
    tibble(
      x = max(.$illuminance),
      y = predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(
        illuminance = max(.$illuminance)
      ))
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(label = as.character(primary))   # 1–6 labels

plot_ee <- ggplot(sim_dat_plot, aes(x = illuminance, y = mean_response, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  # Labels next to fitted lines
  annotate("text", x = 450, y = 315, label = "2", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 540, y = 420, label = "3", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1050, y = 450, label = "4", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 3300, y = 400, label = "5", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 5800, y = 340, label = "6", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 5000, y = 250, label = "7", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_continuous(palette = c("navyblue", "chartreuse3")) +
  labs(x = "Illuminance (lux)",
       y = "Predicted EMG response",
       colour = "Wavelength",
       group = "Primary") +
  theme_minimal() +
  plot_formatting
plot_ee
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plot_medi <- ggplot(sim_dat_plot, aes(x = medi, y = mean_response, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_continuous(palette = c("navyblue", "chartreuse3")) +
  labs(x = "Melanopic EDI (lux)",
       y = "Predicted EMG response",
       colour = "Wavelength\n(nm)",
       group = "Primary") +
  theme_minimal()+
  plot_formatting

plot_hypotheses <- plot_ee + guides(colour = "none") + plot_medi + (labs(y = NULL)) + plot_annotation(title = "Expected results")
plot_hypotheses
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plot_light_metrics <- readRDS(here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "calibration_ee_medi_ratio_1.rds"))
plot_light_metrics

Saving all plots

formatted_save(here("outputs", "figures", "figure_spectra_plot.jpg"), plot = plot_spectra)
saveRDS(plot_spectra, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "figure_spectra_plot.rds"))

formatted_save(here("outputs", "figures", "figure_hypotheses_plot.jpg"), plot = plot_hypotheses)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
saveRDS(plot_spectra, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "figure_hypotheses_plot.rds"))

formatted_save(here("outputs", "figures", "calibration_ee_medi_ratio_1.jpg"), plot = plot_hypotheses)
